originalBehavior: The published article loads through a shared article shell plus the page-local base and light scripts, then initializes many inline lighting, radiance, reflection, and shadow sections under one longform page.
localStatus: The local route keeps the published article structure, shared article styling, and both vendored scripts while rewriting static-hosting metadata and asset paths for the subpath-hosted replica.
notes: The public body no longer exposes the upstream author banner, blog navigation, analytics, or footer supporter prompt.
The route keeps the original longform article structure instead of condensing the content into a custom local rewrite.
evidence: Implementation planning treated the published article shell and both scripts as parity-critical.
Shared smoke coverage verifies the route loads locally, both scripts initialize, and the major lighting sections mount without runtime errors.

originalBehavior: The published article opens with a rectangular-light scene that supports drag rotation and source-size changes, then moves into power and position sections with slider-driven brightness and shading changes.
localStatus: The local route keeps the opening drag scene, the source-position and size controls, and the early power scenes in which slider changes still redraw the emitted-light and shaded-surface visuals.
notes: The opening and early scenes still rely on the original Drawer runtime rather than a local rewrite.
The power and position sections keep the published slider-to-canvas synchronization for emitted power, distance, and angle.
evidence: Implementation planning treated one opening slider scene, one early drag scene, and one power or brightness scene as smoke-critical.
Shared smoke coverage verifies the opening scene redraws from slider input and drag interaction, and an early power scene updates from its published slider.

originalBehavior: The published article continues into radians, steradians, projected solid angles, radiance, reflections, shadowing, color mixing, and bounce-light demonstrations with synchronized geometry and numeric readouts.
localStatus: The local route keeps the projected-solid-angle and radiance sections, plus later mirror, shadow, bounce, and ambient-occlusion scenes driven by the original article runtime.
notes: The later scenes still rely on the original slider and drag behaviors rather than custom local controls.
The route preserves the published order of radiance, reflection, shadow, color, bounce, and sphere-of-light sections.
evidence: Implementation planning treated one projected-solid-angle or radiance scene and one later reflection, shadow, or bounce scene as parity-critical.
Shared smoke coverage verifies a projected-solid-angle scene remains synchronized and a later bounce-light scene redraws from the published slider.

originalBehavior: The published page relies on article-local helper textures and shared control icons while also exposing author promo links, RSS, and supporter surfaces around the article shell.
localStatus: The local copy vendors the helper textures and control sprites locally, keeps the interactive scenes intact, and leaves the shared footer as the only public original-page reference surface.
notes: The local copy uses local fallback font stacks so the route does not depend on runtime Google Fonts requests.
The route does not require upstream media requests at runtime beyond the local helper assets and published scripts.
evidence: The public-surface audit blocks author-promo, analytics, and supporter surfaces from shipping in the route body.
Shared smoke coverage and route checks verify the route loads locally without upstream asset requests while keeping the interactive controls usable.
